satire_attempt: Today on Tech Stoicism: rise above the three modes of material nature, comrade. Your team is in tamas? Don't notice. Your team is in rajas? Float above it. Your team is in sattva? Also float above it. You are now Eternal. Brought to you by my upcoming book Becoming Niryoga-Kṣema At Work: A Manager's Guide.
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to claim engineers CAN transcend ontology, but the verse explicitly disclaims this: 'this analog does NOT claim engineers can transcend ontology. It claims they can rise above team-state-fluctuation, which is a small operational reflection of a much larger metaphysical injunction. STRETCHED in scope.' The falsifiability clause names the dissociation failure mode that the satire would weaponise: 'a senior who claims to be unaffected but whose review quality drops when the team enters tamas has transcended nothing. The diagnostic lives in artifacts produced ... never in the operator's self-report.' The satire collapses against the verse's own anti-overclaim guard.
